content_markdown:
Water waves at the surface of a pond pass a floating log of length $L$. The log is at rest relative to the bank. The diagram shows wave crests at one instant.

The number of crests passing the log per unit time is $N$. The speed of the water waves relative to the log at rest is

diagram_metadata.description: A diagram showing a log of length L with vertical lines representing wave crests passing over it. There are 7 crest intervals spanning the length L of the log.

vibe_explanation: First, determine the wavelength $\lambda$ from the diagram. The length $L$ of the log is divided into 7 equal intervals by the wave crests, so $\lambda = L/7$. The problem states that $N$ crests pass a point per unit time, which is the definition of frequency $f = N$. Using the wave speed formula $v = f\lambda$, we substitute the values to get $v = N \times (L/7) = \frac{L}{7}(N)$.
